title: InstrumentTablatureScore
description: Schema for validating musical instrument tablature structures, supporting multi-voice polyphony, chords, rests, and ties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the musical score.

title.description: The title of the piece or exercise.

instrument.description: The target musical instrument for this tablature (e.g., 'Guitalele').

description.description: An optional summary or stylistic context regarding the arrangement.

timeSignature.description: The time signature of the piece formatted as 'Beats/BeatUnit' (e.g., '4/4', '2/4', '6/8').

measures.description: The chronologically ordered list of bars or measures making up the score.

measureNumber.description: The sequential number of the measure within the score.

notes.description: A collection of sequential or overlapping musical events (notes, rests, chords) within this measure.

duration.description: The rhythmic duration of the musical event in beats (e.g., 4.0 for a whole note, 1.0 for a quarter note, 0.5 for an eighth note in common time).

fret.description: The fret position to press. An integer value means a specific fret, 0 means open string, and null implies a uniquely muted/dead fret scenario or dynamic placement instruction.

string.description: The physical string line index on the instrument fretboard.

voice.description: Used in polyphonic lines to differentiate parallel voice tracks within the same time space (e.g., Voice 1 for melody, Voice 2 for bass line).

tie.description: Indicates that the sound of this note sustains seamlessly into the immediate following note configuration.

description.description: Optional educational text or playback annotations tied to this particular note action.

pitches.description: Defines a simultaneous chord profile. When present, individual string/fret combinations move here instead of the root object mapping.

fret.description: The specific fret position for this piece of the chord array block.

string.description: The target string designation for this coordinate block entry.

allOf.comment: If string is present, fret must be declared too (even if null). Validates standard single notes.
If neither pitches nor string/fret are populated, this object represents a musical rest.
